content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates some perceptual tracking but it remains generic and incomplete. Turn 12 is the clearest visible read — naming that '\"It's fine\" landed a little flat' distinguishes said from shown and changes the conversation. Turn 10 ('Four years is a long time to carry something in your chest') reflects back what the client said but isn't a strong somatic read of the present moment. The coach misses several active reads visible in the transcript: the jaw shifting and eye flicking away (turn 5), the dismissive exhale and the bracing against the somatic question (turn 7), the still moment in turn 11, and the self-doubt-then-armor flip in turn 15. Critically, the coach never names the fight-or-roll-over binary, the aggressive-victim stance, or the rehearsed quality of 'I've looked at this from every angle.' Most questions are blind questions about feelings rather than reads-then-inquiry. One real read (turn 12) plus a near-read (turn 10) against multiple missed in-the-room signals puts this squarely at 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Question about content/hypothetical; no read named. Doesn't track the closed, declarative stance."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good question but blind — no read precedes it. Doesn't name the defendedness already present."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Asks about somatic location without first naming what the coach noticed (jaw shift, eye flick away in turn 5). Read-free somatic question lands as intrusive, which the client confirms."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Content question; misses the brief exhale and the 'this is absurd' energy in turn 7."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the duration and the chest — a soft, partial read of accumulated holding. Generic rather than specifically AoA, but it does land and the client softens."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Clearest read in the conversation: names that 'It's fine' landed flat — distinguishes said from shown. Read precedes question. This is the moment the conversation opens."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "'That lands' is acknowledgment, not a read. Question that follows is good but doesn't name the defensive flip or the aggressive-victim stance."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["fight-or-roll-over-binary", "aggressive-victim-stance", "fear-triangle"],
  "notes": "One solid read (turn 12) and one adjacent reflection (turn 10) against multiple missed somatic and stance-level signals. Pattern-level reads (aggressive-victim, fight-or-roll-over) never surface."
}
